notes: Country-level VIZA RAG seed for philippines. This file is the source of truth for this country's visitor/tourism visa knowledge and is designed to evolve alongside the country-specific form-filling workflow.

documents.title: Philippines Visa-Free Entry or eVisa for Chinese Tourists

chunks.title: Chinese passport tourism route for Philippines
chunks.content: For Chinese ordinary passport holders planning short tourism travel to Philippines, VIZA AI should consider the Philippines visa-free short visitor route when eligible, otherwise eVisa or consular visa route. First confirm nationality, stay length, purpose, hotel booking, return or onward ticket, passport validity, and eTravel status. For Chinese tourists, check the current 14-day visa-free policy and route longer stays to visa/eVisa. Keep advice limited to tourism/visitor travel unless the user states work, study, residence, immigration, journalism, volunteering or another regulated purpose.

chunks.title: Philippines 14-day visa-free entry for Chinese tourists
chunks.content: Philippine government news reports DFA guidance that, effective 16 January 2026, Chinese nationals may enter the Philippines without obtaining a visa for stays up to 14 days for tourism or business visitor purposes. Documents include passport valid at least six months beyond the contemplated stay, confirmed hotel accommodation/booking, and return or onward ticket. VIZA AI should still tell users to check DFA/BI/eTravel rules before departure.

chunks.title: Philippines longer or non-tourist stay route
chunks.content: If the user plans to stay more than 14 days, work, study, reside, or conduct activities outside tourism/business visitor scope, do not present visa-free entry as sufficient. Route them to Philippine visa/eVisa or consular guidance and ask for exact stay length and purpose.

documents.title: Philippines eTravel Arrival Card Intake Requirements

chunks.title: Philippines eTravel channel and form scope
chunks.content: Philippines eTravel is a standalone arrival/departure declaration package, not a 9(a) Temporary Visitor Visa field group. Official source URLs reviewed for this seed: https://etravel.gov.ph/ ; https://customs.etravel.gov.ph/frequently-asked-questions ; https://www.pna.gov.ph/articles/1267062. eTravel registration is free, normally allowed within 72 hours before arrival or departure in the Philippines, and produces an eTravel QR code/reference for boarding and border-health processing. Keep visa/eVisa eligibility and 9(a) consular visa advice in separate Philippines visa packages.

chunks.title: Philippines eTravel fields to collect before filling the form
chunks.content: Collect only the official eTravel declaration data needed for the standalone arrival-card package: personal identity, passport, nationality, country of birth/residence, occupation, contact details, arrival/departure dates, travel type, transport type, flight or vessel details, origin country, port of entry, Philippines address, purpose of travel, health symptoms declaration, baggage/customs declarations, currency declaration, and final traveller declaration.

chunks.title: Philippines eTravel evidence and review checklist
chunks.content: Before submission, review passport data, flight or vessel details, Philippines address, health answers, customs/baggage answers, currency declaration, and final declaration. After successful official submission, preserve the eTravel QR code, reference number, official confirmation page screenshot/download, and portal response summary. Do not treat a normal portal screenshot as success. Do not guarantee entry; Philippine border, health, customs, and immigration authorities make the final decision.
